tools.name: send_calendar_invite
tools.description: Invite a person to a calendar event using only their phone number. They receive the invite as a text message; if they accept, their real email address is returned to the organizer (retrieve it with get_invite_status, or have it POSTed to your callback_url). US and Canada phone numbers only. You must

tools.name: get_invite_status
tools.description: Check whether a sent invite has been accepted or declined, and retrieve the recipient's revealed email once they have accepted. Returns one of: sent, accepted, declined, failed. The revealed email is null until the recipient accepts and provides it.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: send_calendar_invite, get_invite_status · Private data: send_calendar_invite · Public sink: send_calendar_invite, get_invite_status.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: send_calendar_invite, get_invite_status · Private data: send_calendar_invite · Public sink: send_calendar_invite, get_invite_status

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
